name: Ensure VMs are not publicly accessible
description: Virtual machines with public IP paths and SSH or RDP open to the internet.
body:
### Overview

Cyscale fails this control when a VM has a public network path and inbound SSH or RDP is open from the internet. The checks cover AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Alibaba Cloud security rules for ports 22 and 3389.

Management ports should be private and reachable only through bastion, VPN, zero-trust access, or trusted administrative CIDRs.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Remove internet-facing SSH and RDP access from the affected VM.

1. Confirm whether the VM needs a public IP address.
2. Remove the public IP if it is not required.
3. Remove inbound rules for port 22 and 3389 from `0.0.0.0/0`, `::/0`, Internet tags, or empty source lists.
4. Add restricted administrative access through bastion, VPN, private endpoint, or identity-aware access.
5. Re-run sync and assessment.

Provider examples:

```bash
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id <security-group-id> --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 --region {{asset.region}}
az network nsg rule delete --resource-group <resource-group> --nsg-name <nsg-name> --name <rule-name>
gcloud compute firewall-rules update <rule-name> --source-ranges=<trusted-cidr>
```

Cyscale will pass when the VM no longer has public SSH or RDP exposure.
